finding: The irreducible kernel is driven by TORSION/CLASS-NUMBER/SELMER-RANK

interpretation.what_it_is: After removing complexity (Megethos), the dominant residual axis is the ARITHMOS phoneme — torsion for EC, class number for NF, Selmer rank for genus2. These are all measures of ARITHMETIC COMPLEXITY independent of size.
interpretation.why_MF_dirichlet_zero: Modular forms and Dirichlet zeros have no independent torsion/class-number invariant. Their arithmetic is ENCODED in the L-function, not separate from it. So they contribute zero to the residual.
interpretation.the_kernel_equation: Residual PC1 ~ -log(|torsion|) for EC, -log(class_number) for NF, -log(Selmer_rank) for genus2. This IS Arithmos — the phoneme we already named.
interpretation.regulator_connection: In NF, regulator correlates at +0.814 — POSITIVE, while class number is -0.853. The residual kernel captures the class-number-regulator TENSION. These are the two sides of the analytic class number formula: h*R = (stuff involving L-values). The kernel is measuring h vs R.
interpretation.BSD_connection: For EC: BSD says L(E,1) ~ Omega * |Sha| * prod(c_p) * R / |torsion|^2. Torsion appears in the DENOMINATOR. Higher torsion = lower L-value = more constrained curve. The residual axis picks this up at rho=-0.926.

verdict: The irreducible kernel IS Arithmos. The second axis of the Kosmos is the torsion/class-number/Selmer structure that measures arithmetic complexity independent of size. This was already named in the Decaphony. The surgical cuts confirmed it is genuinely independent of Megethos.
